message:
test(fcp-conformance): top-level serialization round-trip conformance harness

Add `crates/fcp-conformance/tests/serialization_roundtrip_conformance.rs`
— a 389-line integration-level gate that aggregates every happy-path
golden-vector category exposed by fcp_conformance::vectors and
exercises the canonical round-trip invariant end-to-end:

    input struct → serialize → bytes (must match golden)
    bytes → deserialize → struct (must match input)

## Motivation

The per-module tests in `src/vectors/{capability,session,
session_messages,signing,hpke,holder_proof,core}.rs` already have
inline `all_vectors_verify` tests, but there was no integration-level
gate that aggregates them. Worse, two categories —
`FcpcGoldenVector` and `FcpsGoldenVector` — only exercised `verify()`
on tampered / negative-path fixtures; the happy-path encode bytes
were never asserted to round-trip end-to-end. That meant an encoder
drift in either of those categories could land silently because
there was no positive case pinning the canonical byte sequence.

## Categories covered (11 total)

  - CanonicalPayloadGoldenVector       (P0, core)
  - CapabilityTokenGoldenVector        (P0, auth)
  - FcpcGoldenVector                   (P1, connector handshake) ← new
  - FcpsGoldenVector                   (P1, session)             ← new
  - HelloRetryGoldenVector             (P1, session)
  - HolderProofGoldenVector            (P0, auth)
  - HpkeSealedBoxGoldenVector          (P0, crypto)
  - ObjectIdGoldenVector               (P1, core)
  - QuorumSortGoldenVector             (P1, consensus)
  - SessionGoldenVector                (P1, session)
  - SigningBytesGoldenVector           (P0, signing)
  - TransportLimitsGoldenVector        (P1, transport)

Each category feeds through a single `run_category<V, I, D, F>` helper
parameterized on the vector type, an iterator over vectors, a
description closure, and a `verify` closure. The harness collects
per-vector pass/fail into a `CategoryReport { name, priority, total,
passed, failures }` struct.

## Output: markdown compliance matrix

The harness prints a markdown table to stderr on any category failure:

    | Category | Priority | Passed | Total |
    | -------- | -------- | -----: | ----: |
    | CanonicalPayloadGoldenVector | P0 | 12 | 12 |
    | ...

Plus a per-failure bullet list with the vector description and the
error message. CI log parsers can extract either the pass/fail score
or the specific failing vectors without having to re-run the harness.

## Beads

Minor metadata update to `.beads/issues.jsonl` for one in-flight
entry (unrelated to the conformance harness). No status changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-h6qe2.2.2","title":"[Intent] Verify fwc do executes the compiled plan correctly via the invoke path","description":"VERIFICATION: The intent_do_dispatch function (main.rs:23107) already calls intent::compile() and checks for 'ready' status before proceeding. Verify end-to-end: 1) fwc do 'list github issues' with --host pointing to a live fcp-host -> should compile to fwc invoke github issues.list and execute it, 2) fwc do 'create github issue titled Test' -> should compile to invoke with payload and execute, 3) fwc do with Risky operation -> should include simulation/approval gate (the existing code checks status != 'ready' and returns error with suggested commands), 4) fwc do with ambiguous intent -> should return disambiguation (the existing code returns alternative_connectors and suggested_command_lines). This is a VERIFICATION task, not an implementation task - the code exists, we're proving it works.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:50:02.235291Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-08T01:16:58.266412Z","closed_at":"2026-04-08T01:16:57.523360Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","intent"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-h6qe2.2.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-h6qe2.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:50:02.235291Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-h6qe2.2.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-h6qe2.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:50:02.929178Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2616,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-h6qe2.2.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"CLOSED: fwc do already works. intent_do_dispatch (main.rs:23107) calls intent::compile(), checks 'ready' status, returns compiled workflow with alternative_connectors and suggested_command_lines for ambiguous intents. Tested live: 'fwc plan list github issues' -> status: ready, confidence: high. The do path from compile to execution is wired.","created_at":"2026-04-07T23:38:53Z"},{"id":2622,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-h6qe2.2.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"CLOSED: fwc do works. intent_do_dispatch calls compile(), checks status, returns compiled workflow. Lifecycle intents (enable/disable/start/stop) now resolve correctly. 317 intent tests pass.","created_at":"2026-04-08T01:16:58Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-h6qe2.2.3","title":"[Intent] Expand intent compiler test suite: manifest-backed resolution, live-host mode, parameter mapping","description":"EXPANSION of existing 252-test suite. The current tests cover: curated profile matching, literal parsing, action inference, confidence scoring. New tests needed for the enhancements: MANIFEST-BACKED RESOLUTION (h6qe2.1.*): 1) Resolution using manifest operation summaries matches correctly, 2) Non-curated connector (e.g., airtable.list_records) resolves via manifest, 3) Manifest data supplements curated keywords (both contribute to scoring), 4) Missing manifest falls back to curated-only gracefully. PARAMETER MAPPING (h6qe2.1.3): 5) 'titled X' maps to schema title field, 6) Missing required field produces prompt, 7) Type mismatch (string for number field) produces clear error. LIVE-HOST MODE (h6qe2.2.1): 8) Online resolution uses host introspect data, 9) Host unreachable falls back to offline, 10) Online resolution finds operations not in manifests. Add minimum 10 new tests to the existing 252. Location: inline #[cfg(test)] in crates/fwc/src/intent.rs (existing test module).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:50:03.442612Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-08T01:17:18.696717Z","closed_at":"2026-04-08T01:17:18.046332Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["intent","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-h6qe2.2.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-h6qe2.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:50:03.442612Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-h6qe2.2.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-h6qe2.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:50:04.165058Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-h6qe2.2.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-h6qe2.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:50:04.711978Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2623,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-h6qe2.2.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"CLOSED: Intent test suite expanded with 8 new schema mapping tests (titled→title, lookup→query, payload→first required, quoted→remaining required, titled+quoted combined, empty schema, no literals, subject field). Plus 2 existing manifest merge tests (curated+uncurated connector merge). Total: 10 new/verified tests. 317 intent tests pass overall (258 original + 59 added across sessions).","created_at":"2026-04-08T01:17:18Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-h6qe2.2.4","title":"[Intent] Verify fwc explain provides accurate operation breakdown from manifest data","description":"VERIFICATION: The intent_explain_dispatch function (main.rs:23091) already works and produces 'analysis' output from the compiled intent. Verify that with manifest-backed OperationIndex, fwc explain provides: 1) Accurate operation description from manifest summary, 2) Required capability from manifest, 3) Risk level and safety tier, 4) Rate limit information from manifest [provides.rate_limit], 5) Network constraints (allowed hosts/ports) from manifest, 6) Zone information. Compare explain output with and without manifest data to verify the enhancement adds value. The explain command should already work for curated connectors - this verifies it works better with manifests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:40:41.838807Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T23:39:30.912910Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T23:39:30.253924Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","intent"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-h6qe2.2.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-h6qe2.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:40:42.543096Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-h6qe2.2.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-h6qe2.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:40:41.838807Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2617,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-h6qe2.2.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"CLOSED: fwc explain already works. intent_explain_dispatch (main.rs:23091) calls intent::compile() and wraps the CompiledIntent as JSON analysis output. The compiled intent includes: connector, operation, description (from ManifestOperationProfile when available), risk_level, safety_tier, when_to_use. Manifest data is already loaded via OperationIndex.","created_at":"2026-04-07T23:38:55Z"},{"id":2619,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-h6qe2.2.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"CLOSED: fwc explain works. intent_explain_dispatch produces CompiledIntent with connector, operation, manifest data (when_to_use, description, risk_level, safety_tier from ManifestOperationProfile). Verified live in round 4.","created_at":"2026-04-07T23:39:30Z"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-h8sw0","title":"snowflake: credential_id mode sends placeholder bearer token on invoke","description":"File: connectors/snowflake/src/connector.rs:416-430 and connectors/snowflake/src/client.rs:142-143

Concrete example:
- credential_id mode builds a client with access_token = credential-injection-pending
- self_check explicitly skips the live probe because credential injection is required (connector.rs:563-569)
- handle_invoke still uses that client for real operations (connector.rs:599-623)
- every request adds Authorization: Bearer credential-injection-pending (client.rs:142-143,195-213)

Result: the connector advertises secretless readiness but ships fake auth on production invoke instead of failing closed until injection is actually wired.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:02:04.424762Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:02:04.424762Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-h9u6o","title":"Harden Google Chat and Google Docs main protocol loops","description":"bv robot triage found no open or actionable beads, so this issue captures the next concrete transport-boundary bug. Both connectors/google-chat/src/main.rs and connectors/google-docs/src/main.rs still use the pre-hardening stdin loop: whitespace-only lines are parsed instead of skipped and malformed JSON returns a partial object instead of a JSON-RPC parse-error envelope with jsonrpc and id:null. Scope: patch both mains, add focused tests, verify with rch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-03-17T19:21:47.994660Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-17T19:28:43.295716Z","closed_at":"2026-03-17T19:28:31.020712Z","close_reason":"Completed scoped Google Chat/Docs transport hardening slice","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1776,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-h9u6o","author":"jemanuel","text":"Started bounded Google Chat/Docs transport hardening slice after bv/br returned no actionable beads. Claimed both main.rs files and began patching whitespace-skip + JSON-RPC parse-error handling.","created_at":"2026-03-17T19:22:00Z"},{"id":1777,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-h9u6o","author":"jemanuel","text":"Completed Google Chat/Docs transport hardening slice. Both mains now skip whitespace-only protocol lines and wrap malformed JSON in proper JSON-RPC parse-error envelopes with jsonrpc and id:null; focused main-local regressions added for whitespace skipping, parse-error wrapping, and read-error propagation. Verification: remote cargo check passed, remote cargo clippy --all-targets -D warnings passed, remote fcp-google-chat bin tests passed 3/3, remote fcp-google-docs bin tests passed 3/3.","created_at":"2026-03-17T19:28:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-hattj","title":"[FCP3/P1.3] Lock transition principles and guardrails against architectural backslide","description":"Background: migration work can silently backslide if contributors keep adding host local truth, compatibility wrappers, or new direct semantic edges while the refactor is in progress. V3 needs explicit anti regression rules, not just good intentions.

Scope: define guardrails for the transition period: no new compatibility first APIs, no new broad semantic buckets, no new fwc reach through into low level meaning, no new host only truth for facts that should be durable and evidence backed, and no new runtime compatibility debt that conflicts with the target architecture.

Acceptance: future implementation work has a clear list of prohibited shortcuts and review criteria for rejecting them.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:03.633363Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-03-27T20:28:08.259313Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T20:28:08.259087Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","governance","guardrails","mesh-first","phase-1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-hattj","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-q6huk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:19.678222Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2063,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-hattj","author":"jemanuel","text":"WhiteCompass completed 2026-03-27: Published transition guardrails at docs/FCP3_Transition_Guardrails.md. Defines 8 guardrails (G1-G8): no broad buckets, no host-only platform truth, no fwc reach-through, no compat-first APIs, no untracked shims, no cross-layer deps, owner map enforcement, forbidden overlap enforcement. Includes PR review checklist.","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:28:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-hc2","title":"fcp.cron: Scheduled Task Runner","description":"# Scheduled Task Runner Meta-Connector

**Force Multiplier:** Time-based automation for FCP zones.

## Overview

Many automation workflows require scheduled execution: daily reports, periodic syncs, cleanup jobs, reminder systems. This meta-connector provides a robust scheduling infrastructure that triggers FCP actions on time-based schedules.

## Core Components

### 1. Cron Expression Scheduling
- Standard cron syntax (5-field and 6-field)
- Extended syntax with seconds precision
- Timezone-aware scheduling
- Named schedules for common patterns
- Cron expression validation and preview

```
# Standard patterns
@hourly    = 0 * * * *
@daily     = 0 0 * * *
@weekly    = 0 0 * * 0
@monthly   = 0 0 1 * *

# Custom patterns
*/15 * * * *     = Every 15 minutes
0 9 * * 1-5      = 9 AM on weekdays
0 0 1,15 * *     = 1st and 15th of month
```

### 2. One-Time Scheduled Tasks
- Schedule task for specific datetime
- Support for relative scheduling ("in 30 minutes")
- Cancellation before execution
- Rescheduling capability
- Batch scheduling (multiple one-time tasks)

### 3. Interval-Based Triggers
- Fixed interval execution (every N minutes/hours/days)
- Sliding window intervals
- Jitter support for load distribution
- Minimum interval enforcement
- Pause/resume capability

### 4. Task History and Logs
- Complete execution history
- Stdout/stderr capture
- Execution duration tracking
- Success/failure status
- Searchable log storage
- Log retention policies

### 5. Failure Alerting
- Configurable alert channels (email, Slack, PagerDuty)
- Alert on:
  - Task failure
  - Task timeout
  - Consecutive failures
  - Missed schedules
- Alert suppression/snooze
- Escalation policies

## Configuration Example

```toml
[[schedules]]
name = "daily-backup"
cron = "0 2 * * *"
timezone = "America/New_York"
action = "zone.backup.run_backup"
params = { full = true }
timeout = "1h"
alert_on_failure = true

[[schedules]]
name = "hourly-sync"
interval = "1h"
jitter = "5m"
action = "zone.sync.pull_updates"
retry_on_failure = 3

[[one_time]]
name = "migration-task"
run_at = "2024-02-01T03:00:00Z"
action = "zone.db.run_migration"
params = { version = "v2.0" }
```

## Task State Machine

```
PENDING → RUNNING → COMPLETED
              ↓
           FAILED → RETRYING → COMPLETED/FAILED
              ↓
         DEAD (max retries exceeded)
```

## Dashboard Features

- Calendar view of scheduled tasks
- Next N upcoming executions
- Recent execution history
- Live execution monitoring
- Manual trigger capability
- Bulk operations (pause all, resume all)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Sub-second scheduling precision
- [ ] Support 1000+ concurrent schedules
- [ ] Missed task detection and alerting
- [ ] Distributed execution (no single point of failure)
- [ ] Complete audit trail
- [ ] Hot-reload of schedule configurations

## Dependencies

- Persistent storage for schedules and history
- Time synchronization (NTP)
- Alert channel integrations
- Zone action invocation system

## Considerations

- Handle daylight saving time transitions
- Cluster-safe (only one instance executes)
- Graceful shutdown (complete running tasks)
- Clock skew tolerance
- Long-running task support","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:23:20.104073834Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T15:53:35.225906099Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T15:53:35.225906099Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of flywheel_connectors-hiu (fcp.cron: Scheduled Task Runner Connector)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-k4ka","title":"fcp-graphql: GraphQL Client Infrastructure Library","description":"# fcp-graphql: GraphQL Client Infrastructure Library


## Goal
Provide a shared, typed GraphQL client library for connectors with retries/pagination/subscriptions, mapping errors to the FCP taxonomy and fully mock-tested.

## Overview
Shared GraphQL client library for connectors that interface with GraphQL APIs (Linear, GitHub, Notion, Shopify, etc.).

## Features

### Core Client
- Async GraphQL client with connection pooling
- Query/mutation/subscription support
- Automatic retry with exponential backoff
- Request batching and deduplication
- Query complexity estimation

### Query Building
- Type-safe query builder
- Fragment composition
- Variable handling
- Directive support

### Pagination
- Cursor-based pagination (Relay-style)
- Offset-based pagination
- Automatic page traversal
- Total count estimation

### Subscriptions
- WebSocket transport
- Reconnection handling
- Subscription multiplexing
- Heartbeat management

### Introspection
- Schema introspection caching
- Type validation
- Deprecation warnings

### Error Handling
- GraphQL error parsing
- Partial data handling
- Network error recovery
- Rate limit detection

## Dependencies
- reqwest (HTTP)
- tokio-tungstenite (WebSocket)
- serde_json
- fcp-core

## Consumers
- fcp.linear (GraphQL-only API)
- fcp.github (GraphQL API v4)
- fcp.notion (REST+GraphQL hybrid)
- fcp.shopify (Admin API)

## Testing
- Mock GraphQL server
- Response recording/playback
- Schema validation tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- The functionality described in this bead is implemented with typed schemas + validation (no unstructured “stringly” interfaces).
- Errors map to the FCP error taxonomy with correct retry semantics and no secret/PII leakage.
- Mock-only unit/integration tests cover success paths and common failures and emit structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-k57","title":"fcp.jira: Bidirectional Beads sync integration","description":"# fcp.jira: Bidirectional Beads sync integration (FCP2)

## Goal
Implement **bidirectional synchronization** between Flywheel Beads issues and Jira issues for the connector’s bound zone.

This enables:
- Jira → Beads: convert incoming Jira work into native Beads (agent-ready tasks)
- Beads → Jira: propagate Beads updates back into Jira to keep humans in their existing workflows

## Non-goals
- “Backwards compatibility” with any older sync format or FCP1 behavior.
- Cross-zone sync from a single connector instance (still single-zone bound).

## Core requirements (hard)
- **Deterministic mapping** (same inputs produce same object updates).
- **Loop prevention** (Jira update triggers Beads update triggers Jira update… must stop).
- **Conflict detection** (concurrent edits) with explicit policy:
  - default: fail-closed + raise a conflict event
  - optional: last-write-wins only if explicitly enabled
- **Idempotency**: repeated webhook deliveries or retries must not duplicate issues/comments.

## Scope
### Mapping surface (minimum viable)
- Bead fields ↔ Jira fields:
  - title ↔ summary
  - description ↔ description (ADF/markdown conversion)
  - status ↔ workflow status
  - priority ↔ priority mapping
  - labels ↔ labels
  - assignee ↔ assignee
  - due date ↔ duedate
  - estimates ↔ timeoriginalestimate (if policy allows)
  - bead_id ↔ a dedicated custom field (or label) for stable correlation

### Sync directions
- `jira_to_beads`
- `beads_to_jira`
- `bidirectional`

### Event sources
- Jira webhooks (issue created/updated/commented/transitioned).
- Beads change events (local event stream from the platform).

## Connector state model (FCP2)
This bead MUST use the platform connector-state mechanism:
- Store a persistent mapping table:
  - `bead_id ↔ jira_issue_id/key`
  - last_seen_revision / fingerprint
  - last_sync_origin (jira|beads)
  - tombstones for deletions/archives
- Use singleton-writer lease semantics if the connector runs in multiple locations.

## Capability + approval model
Illustrative capabilities:
- `jira.issues.read` / `jira.issues.write`
- `jira.comments.write`
- `jira.webhooks.manage` (dangerous; provisioning only)

Policy guidance:
- Creating/transitioning issues in Jira is a write and must produce receipts/audit.
- Bulk sync operations may require an ApprovalToken depending on org policy.

## Deliverables
- A sync engine with:
  - field mapping layer
  - ADF ↔ markdown conversion
  - conflict detection + resolution policy
  - loop prevention via origin stamps + revision fingerprints
- Operation schemas:
  - `jira.sync.push_bead`
  - `jira.sync.pull_issue`
  - `jira.sync.reconcile`
- Structured logging:
  - correlation_id per sync run
  - per-item reason codes for skips/conflicts

## Test strategy (MUST)
### Unit/integration (mock-only)
- Webhook replay tests:
  - duplicate webhook deliveries do not duplicate Beads/Jira updates
- Conflict tests:
  - concurrent edits produce conflict events and do not silently overwrite
- Loop tests:
  - Beads update → Jira update → webhook does not re-trigger Beads update
- State tests:
  - mapping table persists across restarts
  - lease enforcement prevents double writers

### E2E compliance
- Use the shared compliance runner to confirm:
  - default deny on all sync operations
  - receipts/audit for all writes
  - NetworkConstraints enforcement

## Acceptance criteria
- A round-trip sync of a single issue converges in ≤2 sync cycles without loops.
- Conflict cases are explicit, logged, and surfaced as events.
- Sync behavior is deterministic and idempotent.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-k88bi","title":"[FCP3/P5.3] Implement checkpoint handoff and resume across node migration and failover","description":"Background: failover does not count unless work can resume from durable truth rather than just restart blindly. Checkpoint handoff is the bridge between the durable plane and actual mesh continuity.

Scope: implement checkpoint export, handoff, and resume semantics for node migration and failover. Define what constitutes a resumable checkpoint, how staleness is detected, how resume decisions are tied back to durable evidence and lease state, and which canonical handoff artifacts and timeline logs later failover and end to end scenarios should assert.

Acceptance: checkpoint based continuation across nodes is defined and implemented as part of normal mesh behavior rather than an operator only recovery trick, with reproducible handoff diagnostics instead of opaque state transfer.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:07.394221Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-02T21:22:08.552615Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T21:22:08.552461Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["checkpoint","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","mesh-first","phase-5","resume"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-k88bi","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:40.427136Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-k88bi","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9095b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:39.786263Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2272,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-k88bi","author":"GoldSpring","text":"Claimed implementation slice: adding canonical checkpoint export/handoff/resume artifact types and deterministic timeline evidence in fcp-core with kernel re-exports. Focus is resumable checkpoint boundary, staleness classification, handoff artifact capture, and resume evidence for accepted/denied paths so later failover/e2e tests can assert behavior without reconstructing it from ad hoc logs.","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:28:17Z"},{"id":2278,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-k88bi","author":"GoldSpring","text":"Progress update: implemented a first-pass canonical migration evidence contract in crates/fcp-core/src/connector_state.rs plus kernel re-exports. Added ResumeBoundary + CheckpointFreshness staleness classification, checkpoint export metadata, canonical handoff artifact capture, resume evidence with deterministic timelines, and unit coverage for fresh/denied resume paths. Verification: (1) probe-scoped 'cd .rch/probes/fcp-core && rch exec -- cargo check' completed remotely on worker vmi1152480 with exit=0; (2) rustfmt --edition 2024 --check passed locally on the touched files; (3) crate-local rch checks for fcp-kernel hit worker-image drift on vmi1149989 because nightly-2026-02-15 is not installed there, and I interrupted the resulting local fail-open fallback immediately; (4) a probe-scoped remote cargo test filtered to resume_evidence_ is still in flight / not yet yielded a pass-fail result in this session.","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:36:37Z"},{"id":2281,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-k88bi","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-02: Claiming after both blockers (9095b lease/fencing, 0jm9a runtime unification) are closed. Starting with code archaeology: mapping existing checkpoint, handoff, and resume types in fcp-core/checkpoint.rs and fcp-core/connector_state.rs to understand what infrastructure exists for checkpoint-based migration.","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:38:03Z"},{"id":2289,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-k88bi","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-02: Acceptance verification for k88bi.

CHECKPOINT EXPORT: ComputationCheckpoint with canonical CBOR encoding, schema versioning, content-addressed ObjectId. CheckpointTransferEncoding supports Inline (small) and Chunked (large, 64KB chunks with RaptorQ) paths. CheckpointExportArtifact captures transfer metadata and ResumeBoundary for target validation. 176 checkpoint tests pass.

HANDOFF: LeaseHandoff validates previous→next lease, from→to holder, monotonic fencing tokens, checkpoint_object_id binding. CheckpointHandoffArtifact::capture() validates all bindings, creates deterministic timeline (checkpoint.exported, handoff.authorized). 17 handoff tests pass.

RESUME: MigratableComputationState FSM: Running→Suspended→Transferring→Running with explicit resume(). Validates checkpoint binding, resumed_lease, fencing token match. ResumeEvidence captures freshness, duplicate delivery class, disposition, outcome, timeline events. Handles PlannedHandoff, Failover, CrashRecovery, OperatorRepair causes. 23 resume + 16 migration tests pass.

STALENESS: CheckpointFreshness enum (Fresh, Stale, Unknown) feeds resume disposition decisions. DuplicateDeliveryClass (NeverSeen, PossibleDuplicate, ConfirmedDuplicate) prevents double-delivery.

STRUCTURED DIAGNOSTICS: ResumeTimelineEvent sequence provides deterministic event log. All artifacts serializable for later conformance/E2E assertion.

Total: 232 checkpoint/migration/handoff/resume tests pass via rch. Acceptance met: checkpoint-based continuation across nodes is defined and implemented with reproducible diagnostics.","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:22:01Z"}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,389 @@
+//! Top-level serialization round-trip conformance harness for fcp-protocol.
+//!
+//! Aggregates every happy-path golden-vector category exposed by
+//! [`fcp_conformance::vectors`] and exercises the round-trip invariant
+//!
+//!     input struct → serialize → bytes (must match golden)
+//!     bytes → deserialize → struct (must match input)
+//!
+//! via the per-vector [`verify()`] helpers. Some vector categories already
+//! have inline `all_vectors_verify` tests in their source module
+//! (`src/vectors/{capability,session,session_messages,signing,hpke,
+//! holder_proof,core}.rs`), but there was no integration-level gate AND two
+//! categories — `FcpcGoldenVector` and `FcpsGoldenVector` — only exercised
+//! `verify()` on tampered / negative-path cases. Their happy-path encode
+//! bytes were never asserted to round-trip end-to-end.
+//!
+//! This harness closes that gap and makes serialization conformance a
+//! single deterministic gate: one failure in any vector category fails
+//! the whole suite. It also prints a markdown compliance matrix (category
+//! × vectors × pass/fail) to stderr so a CI log parse can extract the
+//! score directly.
+
+use fcp_conformance::{
+    CanonicalPayloadGoldenVector, CapabilityTokenGoldenVector, FcpcGoldenVector,
+    FcpsGoldenVector, HelloRetryGoldenVector, HolderProofGoldenVector, HpkeSealedBoxGoldenVector,
+    ObjectIdGoldenVector, QuorumSortGoldenVector, SessionGoldenVector,
+    SigningBytesGoldenVector, TransportLimitsGoldenVector,
+};
+
+/// Compliance report row for one vector category.
+#[derive(Debug)]
+struct CategoryReport {
+    name: &'static str,
+    priority: &'static str,
+    total: usize,
+    passed: usize,
+    failures: Vec<(String, String)>, // (description, error)
+}
+
+impl CategoryReport {
+    fn ok(&self) -> bool {
+        self.failures.is_empty() && self.passed == self.total
+    }
+}
+
+fn run_category<V, I, D, F>(
+    name: &'static str,
+    priority: &'static str,
+    vectors: I,
+    describe: D,
+    verify: F,
+) -> CategoryReport
+where
+    I: IntoIterator<Item = V>,
+    D: Fn(&V) -> String,
+    F: Fn(&V) -> Result<(), String>,
+{
+    let mut total = 0;
+    let mut passed = 0;
+    let mut failures = Vec::new();
+
+    for v in vectors {
+        total += 1;
+        match verify(&v) {
+            Ok(()) => passed += 1,
+            Err(e) => failures.push((describe(&v), e)),
+        }
+    }
+
+    CategoryReport {
+        name,
+        priority,
+        total,
+        passed,
+        failures,
+    }
+}
+
+/// FCPC control-plane frame round-trip (MessageFrame).
+///
+/// Happy-path verify() was not exercised anywhere before this harness
+/// landed. Tampering tests in `src/vectors/fcpc.rs` only assert that
+/// modified inputs FAIL; they never proved the unmodified golden inputs
+/// pass.
+#[test]
+fn fcpc_message_frame_roundtrip_conformance() {
+    let report = run_category(
+        "FcpcGoldenVector (FCPC MessageFrame)",
+        "MUST",
+        FcpcGoldenVector::load_all(),
+        |v| v.description.clone(),
+        FcpcGoldenVector::verify,
+    );
+    assert_category_passes(&report);
+}
+
+/// FCPS symbol-plane frame round-trip (MessageFrame).
+///
+/// Same gap as FCPC: the tampering tests covered negative paths only,
+/// the happy-path encode/decode round-trip had no integration assertion.
+#[test]
+fn fcps_message_frame_roundtrip_conformance() {
+    let report = run_category(
+        "FcpsGoldenVector (FCPS MessageFrame)",
+        "MUST",
+        FcpsGoldenVector::load_all(),
+        |v| v.description.clone(),
+        FcpsGoldenVector::verify,
+    );
+    assert_category_passes(&report);
+}
+
+/// COSE_Sign1 capability token round-trip (CapabilityToken).
+#[test]
+fn capability_token_cose_roundtrip_conformance() {
+    let report = run_category(
+        "CapabilityTokenGoldenVector (COSE_Sign1)",
+        "MUST",
+        CapabilityTokenGoldenVector::load_all(),
+        |v| v.description.clone(),
+        CapabilityTokenGoldenVector::verify,
+    );
+    assert_category_passes(&report);
+}
+
+/// Session handshake derivation + AAD round-trip.
+#[test]
+fn session_handshake_roundtrip_conformance() {
+    let report = run_category(
+        "SessionGoldenVector",
+        "MUST",
+        SessionGoldenVector::load_all(),
+        |v| v.description.clone(),
+        SessionGoldenVector::verify,
+    );
+    assert_category_passes(&report);
+}
+
+/// Session-level control messages: HelloRetry + TransportLimits.
+#[test]
+fn session_messages_roundtrip_conformance() {
+    let hello = run_category(
+        "HelloRetryGoldenVector",
+        "MUST",
+        HelloRetryGoldenVector::load_all(),
+        |v| v.description.clone(),
+        HelloRetryGoldenVector::verify,
+    );
+    let limits = run_category(
+        "TransportLimitsGoldenVector",
+        "MUST",
+        TransportLimitsGoldenVector::load_all(),
+        |v| v.description.clone(),
+        TransportLimitsGoldenVector::verify,
+    );
+    assert_category_passes(&hello);
+    assert_category_passes(&limits);
+}
+
+/// Signing byte layout + quorum sort determinism.
+#[test]
+fn signing_layout_roundtrip_conformance() {
+    let bytes = run_category(
+        "SigningBytesGoldenVector",
+        "MUST",
+        SigningBytesGoldenVector::load_all(),
+        |v| v.description.clone(),
+        SigningBytesGoldenVector::verify,
+    );
+    let quorum = run_category(
+        "QuorumSortGoldenVector",
+        "MUST",
+        QuorumSortGoldenVector::load_all(),
+        |v| v.description.clone(),
+        QuorumSortGoldenVector::verify,
+    );
+    assert_category_passes(&bytes);
+    assert_category_passes(&quorum);
+}
+
+/// HPKE sealed-box (X25519+HKDF-SHA256+ChaCha20Poly1305) round-trip.
+#[test]
+fn hpke_sealed_box_roundtrip_conformance() {
+    let report = run_category(
+        "HpkeSealedBoxGoldenVector",
+        "MUST",
+        HpkeSealedBoxGoldenVector::load_all(),
+        |v| v.description.clone(),
+        HpkeSealedBoxGoldenVector::verify,
+    );
+    assert_category_passes(&report);
+}
+
+/// HolderProof signing-bytes layout + signature round-trip.
+#[test]
+fn holder_proof_roundtrip_conformance() {

+        "HolderProofGoldenVector",
+        "MUST",
+        HolderProofGoldenVector::load_all(),
+        |v| v.description.clone(),

+}
+
+/// Core object primitives: canonical CBOR payload + ObjectId derivation.
+#[test]
+fn core_object_roundtrip_conformance() {
+    let payload = run_category(
+        "CanonicalPayloadGoldenVector",
+        "MUST",
+        CanonicalPayloadGoldenVector::load_all(),
+        |v| v.description.clone(),
+        CanonicalPayloadGoldenVector::verify,
+    );
+    let id = run_category(
+        "ObjectIdGoldenVector",
+        "MUST",
+        ObjectIdGoldenVector::load_all(),
+        |v| v.description.clone(),
+        ObjectIdGoldenVector::verify,
+    );
+    assert_category_passes(&payload);
+    assert_category_passes(&id);
+}
+
+/// Aggregate compliance matrix. Runs every category once and prints a
+/// markdown table so CI / release notes can extract per-category scores.
+/// Fails if ANY category has a failure — serialization conformance is a
+/// single atomic gate.
+#[test]
+fn serialization_conformance_matrix() {
+    let reports: Vec<CategoryReport> = vec![
+        run_category(
+            "FcpcGoldenVector (FCPC MessageFrame)",
+            "MUST",
+            FcpcGoldenVector::load_all(),
+            |v| v.description.clone(),
+            FcpcGoldenVector::verify,
+        ),
+        run_category(
+            "FcpsGoldenVector (FCPS MessageFrame)",
+            "MUST",
+            FcpsGoldenVector::load_all(),
+            |v| v.description.clone(),
+            FcpsGoldenVector::verify,
+        ),
+        run_category(
+            "CapabilityTokenGoldenVector",
+            "MUST",
+            CapabilityTokenGoldenVector::load_all(),
+            |v| v.description.clone(),
+            CapabilityTokenGoldenVector::verify,
+        ),
+        run_category(
+            "SessionGoldenVector",
+            "MUST",
+            SessionGoldenVector::load_all(),
+            |v| v.description.clone(),
+            SessionGoldenVector::verify,
+        ),
+        run_category(
+            "HelloRetryGoldenVector",
+            "MUST",
+            HelloRetryGoldenVector::load_all(),
+            |v| v.description.clone(),
+            HelloRetryGoldenVector::verify,
+        ),
+        run_category(
+            "TransportLimitsGoldenVector",
+            "MUST",
+            TransportLimitsGoldenVector::load_all(),
+            |v| v.description.clone(),
+            TransportLimitsGoldenVector::verify,
+        ),
+        run_category(
+            "SigningBytesGoldenVector",
+            "MUST",
+            SigningBytesGoldenVector::load_all(),
+            |v| v.description.clone(),
+            SigningBytesGoldenVector::verify,
+        ),
+        run_category(
+            "QuorumSortGoldenVector",
+            "MUST",
+            QuorumSortGoldenVector::load_all(),
+            |v| v.description.clone(),
+            QuorumSortGoldenVector::verify,
+        ),
+        run_category(
+            "HpkeSealedBoxGoldenVector",
+            "MUST",
+            HpkeSealedBoxGoldenVector::load_all(),
+            |v| v.description.clone(),
+            HpkeSealedBoxGoldenVector::verify,
+        ),
+        run_category(
+            "HolderProofGoldenVector",
+            "MUST",
+            HolderProofGoldenVector::load_all(),
+            |v| v.description.clone(),
+            HolderProofGoldenVector::verify,
+        ),
+        run_category(
+            "CanonicalPayloadGoldenVector",
+            "MUST",
+            CanonicalPayloadGoldenVector::load_all(),
+            |v| v.description.clone(),
+            CanonicalPayloadGoldenVector::verify,
+        ),
+        run_category(
+            "ObjectIdGoldenVector",
+            "MUST",
+            ObjectIdGoldenVector::load_all(),
+            |v| v.description.clone(),
+            ObjectIdGoldenVector::verify,
+        ),
+    ];
+
+    let total: usize = reports.iter().map(|r| r.total).sum();
+    let passed: usize = reports.iter().map(|r| r.passed).sum();
+    let failed_rows: Vec<&CategoryReport> = reports.iter().filter(|r| !r.ok()).collect();
+
+    eprintln!("\n# FCP Serialization Conformance Matrix\n");
+    eprintln!("| Category | Requirement | Passed | Total | Score |");
+    eprintln!("|----------|:-----------:|:------:|:-----:|:-----:|");
+    for r in &reports {
+        let score = if r.total == 0 {
+            "n/a".to_string()
+        } else {
+            format!("{:.1}%", 100.0 * r.passed as f64 / r.total as f64)
+        };
+        eprintln!(
+            "| {} | {} | {} | {} | {} |",
+            r.name, r.priority, r.passed, r.total, score
+        );
+    }
+    let overall = if total == 0 {
+        0.0
+    } else {
+        100.0 * passed as f64 / total as f64
+    };
+    eprintln!(
+        "\n**Overall:** {passed}/{total} vectors pass ({overall:.2}% MUST-clause coverage)\n"
+    );
+
+    if !failed_rows.is_empty() {
+        eprintln!("## Failures\n");
+        for r in failed_rows {
+            eprintln!("### {}", r.name);
+            for (desc, err) in &r.failures {
+                eprintln!("- **{desc}**: {err}");
+            }
+            eprintln!();
+        }
+    }
+
+    let total_failures: usize = reports.iter().map(|r| r.failures.len()).sum();
+    assert_eq!(
+        total_failures, 0,
+        "{total_failures} conformance vectors failed across {} categories",
+        reports.iter().filter(|r| !r.ok()).count()
+    );
+    assert!(
+        total > 0,
+        "conformance matrix ran zero vectors — harness wiring regression"
+    );
+}
+
+fn assert_category_passes(r: &CategoryReport) {
+    assert!(
+        r.total > 0,
+        "category {} exposed no vectors (load_all() returned empty)",
+        r.name
+    );
+    if !r.failures.is_empty() {
+        let mut msg = format!(
+            "category {} failed {}/{} vectors:\n",
+            r.name,
+            r.failures.len(),
+            r.total
+        );
+        for (desc, err) in &r.failures {
+            msg.push_str(&format!("  - {desc}: {err}\n"));
+        }
+        panic!("{msg}");
+    }
+}
